'''Mytaranor''' was a bleak [[Factory world/Legends|industrial world]] located in the [[Mytaranor system/Legends|Mytaranor system]], a part of the [[Kashyyyk Region]]'s [[Mytaranor sector/Legends|Mytaranor sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Nightroad/Legends|Nightroad]] linked it to the [[Tholatin system/Legends|Tholatin system]] as well as the [[Terr'skiar sector/Legends|Terr'skiar sector's]] [[Pizilis system/Legends|Pizilis system]].<ref name="TEA" />

A notorious [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]], Mytaranor was best known for its thriving [[Slaver/Legends|slaver]] underground, which included such groups as the [[Mytaranor Slaving Council]]<ref name="TEA" /> that was founded at some point by [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].<ref name="1 BBY">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. The former book establishes that the [[Mytaranor Slaving Council]] existed approximately three years prior to the work's setting, therefore it must have been founded at some point by [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].</ref> At some point, [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]] based at Mytaranor interacted with the [[Bimm (furred species)/Legends|native]] [[Bimm/Legends|inhabitants]] of the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Bimmisaari/Legends|Bimmisaari]],<ref name="TEA" /> and [[Graxol Kelvyyn/Legends|Graxol Kelvyyn]], an [[Anx/Legends|Anx]] slave [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] operating throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="Gamer7">{{GamerCite|7|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Anx}}</ref> around [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="32 BBY">{{GamerCite|7|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Anx}} establishes that [[Graxol Kelvyyn/Legends|Graxol Kelvyyn]] operated as a [[Slaver/Legends|enslaver]] at the same [[Time/Legends|time]] he attended [[Podracing/Legends|Podraces]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. {{Film|I}} depicts Kelvyyn attending the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] Podrace, and ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the film's events to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> associated with a network of slave [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] of which some made their living at Mytaranor.<ref name="Gamer7" /> On the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] [[1 ABY/Legends|36:4:20]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Imperialization/Legends|nationalized]] the [[Megacorporation/Legends|megacorporation]] [[Bansche Tech/Legends|Bansche Tech]]<ref name="Journal6">{{JournalCite|6|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref> on the planet [[Chamble/Legends|Chamble]] on the suspicion that the company was selling [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] components to criminal factions including Mytaranor-based slavers.<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mytaranor was first mentioned in "[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Anx]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] source article published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 7|seventh issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Gamer7" /> on [[October 16]], [[2001]]<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20011018.html|text=Live on the Fringe: ''Gamer'' # 7}}</ref> for use with [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Gamer7" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the world in grid square P-10.<ref name="TEA" />
